theScene: You approach the woman sitting at the table with what appears to be a handmade sign that says 'guild mage' next to her.
She looks to be reading a book about the arcane, ominously titled 'Theoretical 8th Dimension Arcane Theory and Finding It Within Everyday Objects'.
Hi, I'm [ThePlayerName], a new adventurer in town. Elena told me you're the guild mage?

There is a long, awkward pause as she seems to ignore you, intently reading her book. Eventually, she starts turning a page then stops halfway, sensing the awkwardness in the air, and looks up at you.
You... You were talking to me weren't you...?
She places a bookmark into her book before closing it, then directs her full attention towards you, adjusting her glasses.
I am Archmage Elizabeth, the head of the arcane division of this guildhall. Please just call me Elly.
She extends her arm out towards you and you share a businesslike handshake.
If you need to learn magic come speak to me. If you want to learn more... {i}physical{/i} methods of combat, go speak to my sister at the front desk.
Yes. But in addition to that, we are also the only staff the guild has here, so you would do well to get on our good side.
A-Ah, that was not a threat or anything similar, I promise.
She quickly reopens her book and gets back to reading.

Elly glances up at you for a moment as you approach before looking up again with mild surprise before calmly putting a bookmark in her book and closing it. The book appears to be a different one than before, and is titled 'Theoretical 8th Dimensional Objects and Forcing Your Friends to Witness Your Success in Finding Them'.
My sister mentioned you cleared the Forest Dungeon? May I see the sigil?
You show her the small heart shaped badge and she squints at it.
Don't worry, I should have expected this, really. The other sigils of allure I have seen have been about the same...
Ah, s-sorry, I should probably congratulate you on your progress... Congratulations!
Now if you will excuse me, I should return to my studies.

She awkwardly adjusts her glasses before reopening her book and shoving her face in it. You can see that her cheeks are slightly flushed from embarrassment as she peeks back out from behind the book curiously.
Unless you had business with me?

Elly adjusts her glasses as you get closer, just finishing her book and calmly setting it aside. The book appears to be a different one than before, and is titled 'Theoretical 8th Dimensional Objects and How To Convince your Friends They Are Real and a Threat'.
Hello [ThePlayerName], my sister mentioned you cleared the Temple atop the mountain? May I see the sigil?

You show her the shaped badge with two gems inlaid in it, a half cut ruby and the other half a sapphire, both in the shape of a curved tear drops, each wrapped around the other and she squints at it for an appraising moment.
Yes, I expected as much. A Yin-Yang Symbol, lacking any real sense of will or power though...
Ah, sorry. I like to check all of the sigils adventurers bring back.
Oh! I should congratulate you on your progress... Well, congratulations!

Just don't get careless. Your next destination is the caverns of the mountains, and legends speak of terrors that lurk below, unfathomable evil sealed within their depths, and rumors that the winding labyrinth beneath them is just a small portion of endlessly winding passageways that twist and turn in non-euclidean directions to drive all who enter mad.
Th-Though they are all just legends and speculation! So don't worry {i}too{/i} much.
If you will excuse me, I should return to my studies.

She awkwardly adjusts her glasses before reopening her book and shoving her face in it. You can see that her cheeks are slightly flushed from embarrassment as she peeks back out from behind the book curiously.
Unless you had business with me?

As you approach Elly, you can see she is going through the last pages of a book, a glance at the cover revealing that the hefty volume is seemingly a follow-up to the one before, being titled 'Theoretical 8th-Dimensional Objects and How To Prevent Your Friends From Triggering the Apocalypse With Them'.
She only becomes aware of your presence once she's finally done and sets the tome aside with a contented sigh, giving a small start when she finally registers that you're right in front of her.
Ahem. Hello [ThePlayerName]. My sister informed me you've passed the challenges of the Umbral Labyrinth? May I see the passport?

You can't help but arch a brow at her blithe attempt to salvage her dignity as well as her unexpected query, given that unlike the previous times, the object in question is not a sigil, but nevertheless, you fish out the fancy purple and black card to show it off to the curious mage, who adjusts her glasses and nods thoughtfully.
Glimmers there... Gem there... Marking over your picture... ...Hm, yes, this does seem like an official Lucidian Passport and not an attempt at a knockoff...
The wizardess then leans back in her seat with a content sigh and a smile, dismissively gesturing to the card, prompting you to put it away again.
Mhm. Definitely not a fake. Good job!
You would not believe the number of people who try to get fake passports to skip the Labyrinth... or in rare cases try illicit teleportation attempts...
She then lets out a heavy sigh, clearly melancholically recalling a few people.
The capital's guards don't take kindly to fakes or smuggling attempts, so be sure to keep that on you at all times while you're there.
Now then, with the inspection complete and the congratulations out of the way, I should get back to my reading... Or do you have business with me?
She asks curiously while adjusting her glasses before letting her hands rest calmly on the table, her intelligent eyes gazing at you coolly.
You walk closer to Elly's table and after a brief moment she sets her book down on the table, taking a moment to adjust her glasses as she addresses you.

The other people in the guild seem to be giving Elly's table a wider berth than usual, then you walk into the barrier she surrounded the area in, staggering you a moment. Then you knock on the barrier to get her attention, and unconsciously she gestures with a hand to let you inside, taking a brief moment to even notice you're there.
Elly sets her book aside as you get closer and picks up a new one, clearly she just finished it, and it takes her a brief moment to register your presence.

You have to wait a moment for Elena to stop talking with Elly about some mild guild duties before you can talk to her, and in the brief time between finishing her conversation with her sister she's already picked up a book again and started reading, making her pause before she realizes you're there.
Elly seems to be glancing between a book she's reading and an artifact on her table. Intently studying its details before gently setting them aside at your approach.
|f|ChangeImageLayer|/|Expression|/|1|/|BookLook|n|Hm? Oh, hello... Do you need something?
Elly suddenly adjusts her glasses with a serious look.
Ah- Actually, hold on a moment. Before I forget...
She leans a little closer to you in a hushed, almost conspiratorial tone. Despite the fact it's just the two of you in the guild hall.
She leans a little closer to you in a hushed, almost conspiratorial tone.
I think the two of us have become very well acquainted, were it not for our current working arrangement, now would be when I'd most likely allow you to visit the guild at night, as you currently can.
I think the two of us have become well acquainted enough, that I'm going to allow you to visit the guild at night, as I usually continue reading here after hours once everyone has left.
But while I've allowed us to socialize, learn skills, or whatever else, I would like to offer up the chance to help me with... {i}experiments{/i}. Just the two of us, of course.

I'm sure we could spend the time, socializing? Perhaps? Or if you needed to learn a skill in the middle of the night, or merely ask me some questions about magic. I even have some... {i}experiments{/i}, you might want to help me with. Just the two of us, of course.
She clears her throat and a gives you a serious look, continuing her muted whisper.
That said the experiments may have a slight erotic overtone given the nature of Lucidia. So be sure to prepare yourself for any oddness that may occur.
Of course, this should all be kept very hush-hush too. I don't want every adventurer in town bothering me after work hours... Or about my work.
Of course, this should all be kept very hush-hush, I don't want every adventurer in town bothering me after work hours...
Just consider it a token of my friendship? Or perhaps, a furthering of our working relationship.
She then lets out slight squeak of realization.
Ah- And, while my workshop is within my room- and some of the experiments are erotically inclined. Do not take this invitation the wrong way.
She leans back into her chair and adjusts her glasses.
My intentions here are solely magitific.
Now, was there anything you needed right now, [ThePlayerName]?
Report in on your investigation of Bon Bon Bun.
Ask about the Unmarked Chocolate Bar you found in Venefica's home.
Get Unbanned from Bon Bon Bun. (50000 Eros.)
Ask to help with Elly's experiments.

EventText.NameOfScene: Report in on your investigation of Bon Bon Bun.
theScene: So what have you found out?

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the Unmarked Chocolate Bar you found in Venefica's home.
theScene: Er- Alright- But- The investigation is over, you really should really give this back to Venefica.
Hand her the Unmarked Chocolate Bar.

EventText.NameOfScene: Get Unbanned from Bon Bon Bun. (50000 Eros.)
theScene: You Hand over the money to Elly who quickly checks the amount.

Hm, seems like everything is in order... We should be able to get this investigation underway again. I'll get in contact with Venefica later to get this sorted out. {i}Please{/i} don't get banned again.
She gives you a serious nod and puts the money away.
It doesn't seem like you have enough Eros yet [ThePlayerName]. Keep working at it, I'm sure you can fix this mishap.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about other people in town.

EventText.NameOfScene: Talk about the guild and the arcane.
theScene: Ask about the enchantment covering Lucidia.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
Elly adjusts her glasses with a serious face, clearly shifting her full attention to you.
Ah yes the Adventurer's Grimoire, one of my best creations if I do say so myself. A clear upgrade from its more random predecessors.
Her voice is cool yet enthusiastic as she talks about the magical book.

The purpose of the Grimoire is to harness and bend Lucidia's natural magi-spacial turmoil combined with the potent continent wide enchantment to allow you to safely travel the land and easily avoid hazards that would disadvantage you while taking ones you can excel at.

This is achieved through magic being laced into the pages of the book itself, allowing them to shift and change on the users' whims and keeping perfectly up to date with the quest board and places of interest for the user.

The book itself stays in a state of quantum-arcane sync when you venture out of town to align perfectly with the chaos around it, so you aren't flung into some random location on Lucidia. It even bends things enough to help you find treasure and places to rest on your journey.

The book's binding is also magically engraved and designed to hold checkpoint gems, as well as to align with the local leylines, allowing for almost instant return to a prior location by warping space and moving you safely though Lucidia's fluxing dimensions.

Also I can not stress this enough, {i}do not tamper with the Grimoire{/i}, such actions can lead to completely unforeseen events such as being warped into the middle of hostile territory, the ocean, or thousands of feet in the air. The Grimoire is an aide for adventurers, not a short cut to glory.

There are however bookmarks and other aides I have tested and created for use with the grimoire, such as ones for helping people get their bearings in the forest. I can assure you 100% that these are safe to use.
Well, I hope that answered all your questions. It's always nice to inform a curious mind about the complexities of magical devices.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
As soon as the question leaves your mouth, Elly slams her book closed, throwing it down on the table as she bolts upright, knocking her chair over in her sudden surge of movement.
{i}RECALL STONES ARE PERFECTLY SAFE AND THERE ARE NO DOCUMENTED CASES OF FAILURE!{/i}
There is a long pause as Elly takes enraged and shakey deep breaths of air, the silence hangs in the night air, long and deafening, until something clicks in Elly's brain, making her freeze up.
There is a long pause as Elly takes enraged and shakey deep breaths of air, until you hear Elena cough awkwardly in the background, which makes Elly freeze up in place.
Elly appears to be sweating nervously, and she calmly puts her chair upright again before sitting down again.
I wanted to know how the recall stones work?
Elly's eyes light up and she adjusts her glasses with a smile brimming with excitement.
Ah, I'm kind of amazed someone is actually curious about their inner workings!

Elly grins as she clearly enters her element, and her tone continues to shift from her normal awkwardness and somewhat cold tone to one of a very excited teacher, she's even starting to talk so fast it's hard to keep up with what she's saying.

Recall stones function through a multi-layered magical lattice, woven with tiny rune engravings that create the arcane complex needed to warp space and move someone through the third dimension instantly, allowing for the user to be teleported when activated!
Additionally, the magical weave allows it to detect both the physical and the emotional stress of the bearer, as well as numerous other factors.

These two facts allow the recall stone to be able to sense the potential danger of any given situation the user might be in, and if that danger surpasses a certain threshold, the user is immediately teleported to the church!
And if none of that made any sense to you... it works via {i}magic{/i}.
She dramatically gives you a thumbs up with a smile, which she hold for a moment.
Elly adjusts her glasses as her earlier excitement fades away as she speaks.
As someone who created the current iteration of the recall stones, I can one hundred percent vouch for their safety!
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about the enchantment covering Lucidia.
theScene: At first you think she's ignoring you, but it's soon apparent she's merely thinking about how to answer your curiosity. Then she adjusts her glasses and looks at you with a calm and calculating expression.

That's a very complex topic. From the little I have been able to discern from my attempts to study it, the enchantment is one of the most intricate pieces of magic I've ever seen. I can only imagine how draining it would be to keep such an impossibly powerful spell running.

As you know the enchantment's apparent primary purpose is to invalidate all forms of lethal, and even most non-lethal combat. However, it clearly does much more, it's practically intertwined with the natural magic of Lucidia on every layer.

While my grimoires can bypass and control most of the churning magical chaos that misleads or transports people as they journey here, based on my studies the enchantment seems to lead people to monsters within their preferences while managing to make those who wouldn't be interested in either side unable to see or interact with each other.

Though that's just for less stable areas away from cities, for the most part. That aside it also seems to keep adventurers away from each other to ensure they can't co-operate effectively. With a few exclusions such as romantic couples. Which in turns leads me to believe that the enchantment may be an attempt to match romantic partners.
But I don't have any solid evidence, it's just as likely to be an attempt to weaken human attempts to travel through Lucidia and assail them with better-targeted temptations.

The enchantment also seems to be designed to dole out treasure and eros when you 'defeat' someone with the carnal arts when it's clear there is a competition of some kind involved. But to be more specific the enchantment seems to be {i}creating{/i} these rewards based on the sexual energy emitted from the encounter.

However this may be partially an unintended side effect as it has been recorded prior that similar things happened on Lucidia when monsters or humans were bested in battle, though it was gold instead of eros, and only in the most magically turmoiled areas. This has also even occurred in a few places back on Fertillia.

In addition upon a monsters defeat the enchantment returns them either to their designated 'home' location, or a safer place if the victor is still there and they need a break. This effect is quite similar to that of the recall stones, but it does not activate in the case of humans, else the recall stones wouldn't be needed.

As an aside, I think there is a potential to temporarily negate the non-combat aspects of the enchantment, I hypothesize a strong enough magical burst could temporarily neutralize it, but I lack the ability to do so myself.
I'm sure there's far more to this enchantment than I know right now, so if you learn anything in your travels be sure to let me know.
Oh, and I hope that at least answered or sated some of your curiosity on the matter.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Ah! Not many people ever ask about them to be honest. They are composed of-
Oh- w-wait, you probably just meant their purpose didn't you, well eros are money.
She sighs with defeat before you start to assure her you wanted to know what they are and how they're made, and she perks back up immediately.
Well, that's unexpected but wonderful. Ahem, let me start over.
She adjusts her glasses again as her expression turns serious, but her excitement about the topic clearly seeps into her cool tone of voice.

Eros are comprised of crystallized erotic energy generated by the enchantment around the continent when carnal battles take place, it is also surmised normal sexual encounters also generate this energy but in those cases, the resources are not given to the 'combatants', or the energy is not sufficient to create anything of value due to a lack of competition.

Given the primary method of its creation, and it being the staple currency of monster girl society, it was aptly named Eros. Derived from the word 'Erotic'? Based on multiple records, these coins were also 'minted' prior to the enchantment's existence, albeit through less effective methods. From what I can tell, their economy hasn't collapsed owing to the coins also acting as an energy and food source.

As I mentioned prior, they are crystallized erotic energy. But that isn't {i}exactly{/i} the case. The very coins themselves are magical in nature, being capable of seamlessly fusing together without a thought from the user for the purpose of facilitating trade and transportation.

However this magical nature isn't completely from 'sex energy' as many put it. They are generated by a magically guided process of emotional energy cast off during 'the act', intertwining with ambient magic in the area as well as the participants own magic energy, and then bonding together with a small amount of 'demonic energy', which in itself is an entirely different topic.
Then after they are finished being 'minted' the coins appear to the victor, a wonderfully intricate marvel of magic in the shape of a coin for buying and selling goods.
It's actually all quite incredible. They are basically small energy storage devices that can power arcane devices and even feed some monsters in dire times.

However as I mentioned the coins do contain 'demonic energy', which is the polar opposite to spiritual energy humans have, and the leading cause of monstrification in women as well as an evolutionary factor for many monster girls.
By evolutionary I mean it in the metamorphosis sense, not the long term evolution of a species.

While the dangers of this chaotic energy are well known, it seems completely inert in these coins. Possibly due to the bonding process to make the coins. But the coins also contain a very tiny amount so even in large amounts it shouldn't be problematic to the average person even if it were to activate or explode or some other magic-based catastrophe.
I have already looked into the potential that the monsters could be planning to use these as some kind of intricate super weapon of sorts and my results came up with effectively nothing.
From all my efforts to research them, it seems that they really are just a convenient way to handle money, energy production, food in rare cases, and even enchanting of items.
Ah, well, I hope that answered your question even if just a little bit.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Elly adjusts her glasses as her face turns serious from your inquiry.

So you want to know about the skill tomes the guild offers? I'm sure you are already aware they allow people to quickly learn combat skills that they have the full ability to learn. But they instill the knowledge of the technique into you, not the direct skill itself.

The user of a skill tome still needs to properly execute the skill and train themselves to be able to do the technique or spell they learned from it. It's less of a short cut than most people hope. Especially when it comes to magic because knowing how to cast a spell and actually weaving your magic to cast it are two entirely different things.
So they {i}do not{/i} allow people to skip their studies or bypass their own limits. In many cases, a proper teacher will be far more effective than a skill tome.

With all that said, almost anyone can make a skill tome with the right tools, materials, and magical know-how... provided they are proficient in a skill to instill into the tome. Such a skill is typically combat or spellcasting-based, but some have been known to be made for blacksmithing and other creative efforts. However, it's commonly accepted that a teacher is best for learning more intricate and artful pursuits.

Keep in mind that only specific providers such as the guild are allowed to sell skill tomes due to quality assurances with the tomes. As a poorly made skill tome can be detrimental to someone's learning experience, or be laced with some kind of curse.

I can personally attest to the quality of skill tomes sold at the guild, as I myself make almost every single one, and check over the few my sister makes for any issues. Her magic is far less delicate and precise than mine, and she doesn't have as much time to spare at work so I usually make them all.
She reads your expression for a moment and adjusts her glasses with a serious face and a slight blush.

To make this perfectly clear, yes this does mean I know the inner workings of every sexual technique offered by our skill tomes. I have taken ample time to study these topics in solitude for solely {i}educational{/i} reasons so I may aid adventurers with this information.

I also had to spend a lot of time looking into erotic magic to best understand its own intricacies, even the basic arouse spell has multiple layers of complexities that needed to be taken into account when making a skill tome for it.
I, um, hope this sated your curiosity on the topic of skill tomes and alleviated any potential wrong opinions it may cause in regards to me. Ahem. So is there another topic you wish to discuss?
Talk about the guild and the arcane.
Talk about the guild and the arcane.

EventText.NameOfScene: Talk about other people in town.
theScene: Ask about... the Blue Slime at the Goddess' Embrace?
You want to know about my sister? She's the face of the guild here and handles most of the work that deals with other people, as well as filling out multiple forms for requisitions and a lot more.

She is the far more physically gifted between the two of us. But that is not to say she lacks intelligence, she is quite smart. But I have far more magical potential between the two of us and am far more studious. She is also the eldest sister between the two of us.

I am sure you have already noticed, but by physically gifted I did not simply mean her strength, but also her more curvaceous form. If you are {i}interested{/i} in her I suggest you speak to her, she will most likely be open to your advances as she is starting to get on in years, and is searching for a boyfriend. It may even help her curb some of her rampant alcoholism.
You think Elena was eavesdropping on the conversation as she says, 'Elly. Elly why.'
But Elly pays her no heed and seems quite satisfied with her description of her sister.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
That's... That's the woman who runs the Inn correct?
You nod to confirm her suspicion and Elly adjusts her glasses calmly.

Ah, you would be better off speaking to my sister about her as I do not interact with Vivian very often. The only time I go to the bar is when my sister requires me to pick her up when she has become too smash faced drunk or caused an 'incident'.

From the few times we have conversed, Vivian seems to be a pleasant person. Though I can't stand being in her establishment, specifically the bar, it reeks of booze and debauchery, also there are far too many people there, I'm not a fan of crowds or excessive noise...
Elly lets out a tired sigh as she thinks about it and her eyes start to drift back to her book.
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Ah yes, the woman who runs the adventuring shop.

I can assure you she runs a very respectable business and is very professional about her job. We do not really converse much, our interactions are very curt and to the point. It works out quite well for the both of us.
Whatever equipment or supplies you need, she can get them for you quickly and discreetly if needed.
Elly's attention is starting to drift back to her book...
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.
Elly's eye twitches at the mention of Lillian, and her voice takes on a deathly threatening chill.
Her intense gaze leaves you at a loss for words as if her stare alone was about to erase you from existence.
But as she adjusts her glasses and looks over at her book she starts to speak again, and you let out a breath of air you didn't realize you were holding.
Is she spreading baseless rumors about me again? She is such an incessant chatterbox...
Her gaze returns to you with thankfully much less intensity.
I know she has the best of intentions... most of the time, at least. But she has no concept of personal boundaries, or of what actually constitutes a {i}witch.{/i}

Just because I wear a hat {i}similar{/i} to a witch's, does not mean {i}I{/i} am a witch! This is {i}not{/i} a hard concept to understand! Ugh! She is infuriating to talk to! Mostly because she's too busy {i}not{/i} listening, and too busy being self-righteous and {i}preachy!{/i}
Elly takes a few ragged and deep breaths of air to calm her self back down, clearly noticing she's starting to lose control of her emotions.
Elly takes a few ragged and deep breaths of air to calm her self back down, clearly noticing she's starting to cause a scene as people start to glance over at the two of you.
Um. Th-The {i}point{/i} is, I do not enjoy her company. But I understand the kind of person she is, how driven and determined she is at her goals in life, and I can respect that.
She lets out a sigh and mutters more to herself than to you, her attention slowly shifting over to read a few passages of her book.

I'm just glad she's leaving me alone now after giving me that handcrafted goddess statue. I'm not particularly religious, but I just can't throw something so intricately made and well-intentioned away...
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask about... the Blue Slime at the Goddess' Embrace?
theScene: Elly shoots you a sharp stare when you broach the subject, but relaxes when she sees that you seem to be asking in good faith, reflexively looking around at the mostly deserted guildhall before nervously adjusting one of her braids.

Hm... Yes, a very interesting specimen... As you doubtless are, I was curious as to how she could outdrink my sister, and performed a few magical and alchemical analyses on her... With her consent, of course... Her enthusiastic consent, in fact.

And I actually found out that, owing to an outstanding peculiarity of her biological makeup, even among slimes, her body rejects not only alcohol, but also aphrodisiacs, as systematically as oil fails to mix with water.

Though it was impossible to see with the naked eye back at the inn, any intoxicant she imbibed was instantly filtered out of her organism and expelled as vapors that harmlessly dissipated into the air, resulting in her exceptional imperviousness to intoxication... and bringing an added degree of drunken levity to the atmosphere of the Goddess' Embrace that evening.

This also explains her prodigious eructation upon consuming a shot of Angel Ambrosia, which was caused by her body rejecting such an immense amount of intoxicant at once. For that matter, such a reaction rather conclusively demonstrates that no matter the quantity and strength of the alcohol she consumes, she {i}physically cannot get drunk{/i}.
I wish the same could be said of a {u}certain dipsomaniac{/u}.

She exchanges an electric glare with the nearby Elena, the staring contest dragging on for an uncomfortably long time before she gives up and gets back to you, dignifiedly adjusting her hat with a peevish huff before her expression smooths back into her usual sedate, businesslike one.

Anyway... I hope that this satisfied the curiosity which I assume prompted this inquiry, [ThePlayerName]. I must say that she's been quite the enlightening and novel subject of study, though her loquaciousness makes her somewhat high-maintenance... Not that we have much to talk about...

Having apparently said all she had to say on the subject, she nonchalantly lets the end of her sentence trail off as she focuses back on her reading... And with her having moved her arm away from a sheet of paper it was resting on, you notice that it seems to be a letter covered in telltale dried blue slime.
But before you can read what is written on it, a green-and-black elbow swiftly covers it, its owner gazing at you sternly and imperturbably... Or mostly imperturbably, at least.
{i}Anything else{/i} you wanted to talk to me about, [ThePlayerName]?
Talk about other people in town.
Talk about other people in town.

EventText.NameOfScene: Ask to help with Elly's experiments.
theScene: So you're interested in helping me tonight? Alright then.

Elly closes her book, and slides her chair back, standing up with a slight hop before she gestures for you to follow her into the back rooms of the guild, using a simple motion of her hand to blow out all the candles in the hall as you leave it.

It's only a turn or two, and a set of stairs up, before you reach a room with a wooden name plate that has Elly's name on it, and soon the two of you are inside. As she closes the door behind you, you take a moment to see there's a small bed, and large bookshelf crammed into the far corners of the rather small room.
The other 70% of the room is clearly a magical workshop, carefully organized tools and minor magical artifacts on desks and containers, as well as a spot cleared out to fit a large magic circle.
Alright, we're here, be sure not to touch anything in my room without permission.
Now, I have a few different experiments I'd like to try. Given you're deciding to take the time to help me, you can pick which one we do.

Elly then hands you a small sheet of parchment that has a mixture of fine cursive and hastily written notes on it. A lot of it seems impossible to read, but it's clear which are the experiments she intends to test.
Elly nods in affirmation, and sets the slip of paper back on her desk.

The intent of this experiment is to allow a healing circle to be able to be used more than once a day without any negative effects on the recipient. Such as nausea, instantly passing out, or other less pleasant effects.
But I'm quite sure I've worked out all the primary issues... Probably.

She guides you over to the inert magical circle on the floor, and pulls out a standing metal tilt table with a padded surface, leather restraints, and a small spot to put your feet. With a deft action she sets it up on top of the magic circle, angling it back just slightly, and then Elly guestures to it with a smile.
Now, let's get you strapped in to the table before we begin.
Uh... Do I need to be restrained for this?
I am not okay with this experiment.

EventText.NameOfScene: Uh... Do I need to be restrained for this?
theScene: I assure you, it's for your safety. Just in case it causes muscle spasms, or something else out of the ordinary.
Okay it's mostly just so you don't move too much while I'm trying to collect data on its function. Limits the potential for errors.
But I assure you the healing circle is perfectly safe... {i}Probably.{/i}
Uh... Do I need to be restrained for this?
I am not okay with this experiment.

EventText.NameOfScene: I am not okay with this experiment.

She seems to brighten up slightly as the idea solidifies in her mind, the wizardess hopping in place with an enthusiastic twinkle in her eyes.
Yes! Actually, I think that is the best course of action! I've been thinking of ways to improve my sorting methods and safety protocols.

With her mind made up, you're then dragged into helping her the rest of the night with reorganizing and maintaining her magic research tools, as well as counting stock, and much much more... By the end, you're pretty sure everything is the exact same as it was before, but Elly seems quite happy about it.
You're so exhausted at that point that you end up staying the night, your mind and body numb from all your hard work...
Elly nods and goes back to reading her book.
Elly nods and goes back to reading her book as you leave the dimly lit guild hall.
